gaps.snippet: # [doc = " An immutable, content-addressable UTF-8 filesystem path (C4 / ADR-003)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " A `Path` is a **value**: two `Path`s with the same string are the same value regardless of"] # [doc = " where they were constructed. Metadata (mtime, inode) is NOT part of its identity."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG (Q3 — portability)"] # [doc = " Currently backed by a `String` (UTF-8 guarantee). Non-UTF-8 OS paths are an open question"] # [doc = " (spec §7-Q3 / RFC-0016 §8-Q3); a lossy decode would violate C1 — the maintainer's call."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # PATH SEPARATOR"] # [doc = " Component separator is `/`. No implicit OS-specific normalization (no `\\\\` on Windows)."] # [doc = " This is a conservative default pending the Q3 portability decision."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq , Hash)] pub struct Path { inner : String , }
gaps.reason: struct `Path` uses named fields — no record/product-type surface (only a single-ctor positional shape maps to `type_item`)

gaps.snippet: impl Path { # [doc = " Construct a `Path` from a UTF-8 string slice."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact`, total"] # [doc = " Accepts any UTF-8 string. Currently no normalization is applied (conservative; see FLAG Q3)."] # [doc = " An empty string is a valid path (the empty / relative path)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: none (pure lexical; no syscall)"] # [doc = " # FLAG (Q3): non-UTF-8 OS paths are not handled; pending ratification."] # [must_use] pub fn new (s : impl Into < String >) -> Self { Self { inner : s . into () } } # [doc = " The path as a string slice."] # [must_use] pub fn as_str (& self) -> & str { & self . inner } # [doc = " Lexically join a child component onto this path."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " A `/`-separated join: `join(\"/foo\", \"bar\")` = `/foo/bar`."] # [doc = " If `child` is an absolute path (starts with `/`), it replaces `self` (POSIX convention)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact`, total"] # [doc = " Pure lexical computation; no syscall, no normalization beyond joining."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] # [must_use] pub fn join (& self , child : & str) -> Self { if child . starts_with ('/') { Self :: new (child) } else if self . inner . is_empty () || self . inner == "/" { let mut s = self . inner . clone () ; if ! s . ends_with ('/') { s . push ('/') ; } s . push_str (child) ; Self { inner : s } } else { Self { inner : format ! ("{}/{}" , self . inner . trim_end_matches ('/') , child) , } } } # [doc = " The parent directory of this path, or `None` at the root."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `parent(\"/foo/bar\")` = `Some(\"/foo\")`."] # [doc = " `parent(\"/\")` = `None` (at the root; nowhere to ascend)."] # [doc = " `parent(\"foo\")` = `None` (relative single component; no parent in this path string)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (total, returns `Option` — `None` is explicit, not a sentinel)"] # [doc = " # Effects: none (pure lexical)"] # [must_use] pub fn parent (& self) -> Option < Self > { let s = self . inner . trim_end_matches ('/') ; if s . is_empty () || s == "/" { return None ; } match s . rfind ('/') { None => None , Some (0) => Some (Self :: new ("/")) , Some (idx) => Some (Self :: new (& s [.. idx])) , } } # [doc = " The final component of the path (the file/directory name), or `None` for root."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact`, total (returns `Option`)"] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] # [must_use] pub fn file_name (& self) -> Option < & str > { let s = self . inner . trim_end_matches ('/') ; if s . is_empty () { return None ; } match s . rfind ('/') { None => Some (s) , Some (idx) => { let name = & s [idx + 1 ..] ; if name . is_empty () { None } else { Some (name) } } } } # [doc = " Whether this path starts with `/` (i.e. is an absolute path)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact`, total"] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] # [must_use] pub fn is_absolute (& self) -> bool { self . inner . starts_with ('/') } }

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; # [doc = " `Path::new` is pure and total: any UTF-8 string is accepted."] # [doc = " Guard: panicking on any UTF-8 string makes this fail."] # [test] fn path_new_is_total () { let _ = Path :: new ("") ; let _ = Path :: new ("/") ; let _ = Path :: new ("/foo/bar") ; let _ = Path :: new ("relative") ; } # [doc = " `Path` is value-semantic: two paths with the same string are equal."] # [doc = " Guard: path identity using pointer equality makes this fail."] # [test] fn path_equality_is_value_semantic () { let a = Path :: new ("/foo/bar") ; let b = Path :: new ("/foo/bar") ; assert_eq ! (a , b , "paths with equal strings must be equal (C4)") ; } # [doc = " Different strings → different paths."] # [doc = " Guard: a constant hash that ignores the string makes this fail."] # [test] fn different_strings_are_different_paths () { let a = Path :: new ("/foo/bar") ; let b = Path :: new ("/foo/baz") ; assert_ne ! (a , b) ; } # [doc = " join appends a relative child with a slash separator."] # [test] fn join_appends_relative_child () { let p = Path :: new ("/foo") ; let q = p . join ("bar") ; assert_eq ! (q . as_str () , "/foo/bar") ; } # [doc = " Joining an absolute child replaces the base (POSIX convention)."] # [doc = " Guard: concatenating regardless of child absoluteness makes this fail."] # [test] fn join_absolute_child_replaces_base () { let p = Path :: new ("/foo") ; let q = p . join ("/absolute") ; assert_eq ! (q . as_str () , "/absolute") ; } # [doc = " join from root produces a clean path (no double slash)."] # [test] fn join_from_root_no_double_slash () { let p = Path :: new ("/") ; let q = p . join ("bar") ; assert_eq ! (q . as_str () , "/bar") ; } # [doc = " join is associative: `p.join(\"a\").join(\"b\")` == `p.join(\"a/b\")`."] # [doc = " Guard: a non-associative implementation makes this fail."] # [test] fn join_associativity () { let p = Path :: new ("/root") ; let two_steps = p . join ("a") . join ("b") ; let one_step = p . join ("a/b") ; assert_eq ! (two_steps , one_step , "join must be associative") ; } # [doc = " `parent` returns the parent directory for a multi-component path."] # [test] fn parent_returns_parent_of_nested_path () { assert_eq ! (Path :: new ("/foo/bar") . parent () , Some (Path :: new ("/foo"))) ; } # [doc = " `parent` at root returns `None` (not a sentinel)."] # [doc = " Guard: returning `Some(\"\")` instead of `None` makes this fail."] # [test] fn parent_at_root_returns_none () { assert_eq ! (Path :: new ("/") . parent () , None , "root has no parent") ; } # [doc = " `parent` of a single-level absolute path returns root."] # [test] fn parent_of_single_level_absolute_returns_root () { assert_eq ! (Path :: new ("/foo") . parent () , Some (Path :: new ("/"))) ; } # [doc = " `parent` of a relative single-component path returns `None`."] # [test] fn parent_of_relative_single_component_returns_none () { assert_eq ! (Path :: new ("foo") . parent () , None) ; } # [doc = " `parent` of an empty path returns `None`."] # [test] fn parent_of_empty_returns_none () { assert_eq ! (Path :: new ("") . parent () , None) ; } # [doc = " `file_name` returns the final path component."] # [test] fn file_name_returns_final_component () { assert_eq ! (Path :: new ("/foo/bar.txt") . file_name () , Some ("bar.txt")) ; } # [doc = " `file_name` of root returns `None`."] # [test] fn file_name_of_root_returns_none () { assert_eq ! (Path :: new ("/") . file_name () , None) ; } # [doc = " `file_name` of an empty path returns `None`."] # [test] fn file_name_of_empty_returns_none () { assert_eq ! (Path :: new ("") . file_name () , None) ; } # [doc = " Absolute paths start with `/`."] # [test] fn is_absolute_for_slash_prefixed () { assert ! (Path :: new ("/foo") . is_absolute ()) ; assert ! (Path :: new ("/") . is_absolute ()) ; } # [doc = " Relative paths are not absolute."] # [test] fn is_not_absolute_for_relative () { assert ! (! Path :: new ("foo") . is_absolute ()) ; assert ! (! Path :: new ("") . is_absolute ()) ; } # [doc = " Joining the same child always produces the same result (deterministic — Exact)."] # [doc = " Guard: any nondeterminism in join makes this fail."] # [test] fn join_is_deterministic () { let base = Path :: new ("/base") ; for child in & ["a" , "b" , "c/d" , "e/f/g"] { assert_eq ! (base . join (child) , base . join (child) , "join must be deterministic") ; } } # [doc = " Parent and join are inverse for well-formed paths: `parent(p.join(c)) == p`."] # [doc = " Guard: a non-inverse pair makes this fail."] # [test] fn parent_is_inverse_of_join_for_simple_components () { let base = Path :: new ("/root/sub") ; let joined = base . join ("file.txt") ; assert_eq ! (joined . parent () , Some (base . clone ()) , "parent(base.join(child)) must equal base for a single-component child") ; } }
